title: Myrsine howittiana
taxonomy: Myrsine howittiana is classified within the family Myrsinaceae. This family is part of the order Ericales. The order Ericales belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subgroup of the larger clade of flowering plants called angiosperms. The class Magnoliopsida also includes other families such as the Clethraceae and the Ericaceae. Myrsine howittiana's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Myrsine howittiana, also known as the mountain guava, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of M. howittiana include myrsinol, scopoletin, and other lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, M. howittiana is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used to make a decoction, and is also sometimes used in the production of herbal extracts and tinctures.
